project.name: know-cli
project.description: Product knowledge graph CLI for AI-driven software development

solo-developer.name: Solo Developer
solo-developer.description: Primary user: solo technical lead (including teams-of-one) doing rapid prototyping with AI assistance

ai-agent.name: AI Agent
ai-agent.description: Secondary user: AI agents (Claude, GPT) consuming structured graph context

structure-product-context.name: Structure Product Context
structure-product-context.description: Convert development conversations into queryable dependency graphs

generate-ai-specs.name: Generate AI Specs
generate-ai-specs.description: Produce specifications that AI agents understand without token waste

track-implementation-progress.name: Track Implementation Progress
track-implementation-progress.description: Link features to code and track completion status

build-features.name: Build Features
build-features.description: Use graph to guide feature development

be-source-of-truth.name: Be Source of Truth
be-source-of-truth.description: Make graph the authoritative product spec

query-graph-intelligence.name: Query Graph Intelligence
query-graph-intelligence.description: Get product context via structured queries

maintain-tool-quality.name: Maintain Tool Quality
maintain-tool-quality.description: Keep codebase healthy, add features, fix bugs

graph-management.name: Graph Management
graph-management.description: CRUD operations, migration, deprecation, phase management

graph-validation.name: Graph Validation
graph-validation.description: Multi-level validation: syntax, structure, semantics, health, cycles

spec-generation.name: Spec Generation
spec-generation.description: Generate markdown and XML specs from graph entities

feature-lifecycle.name: Feature Lifecycle
feature-lifecycle.description: Track features via contracts, git integration, drift analysis

graph-visualization.name: Graph Visualization
graph-visualization.description: Multi-format rendering: tree, mermaid, dot, HTML, fzf

impact-analysis.name: Impact Analysis
impact-analysis.description: Analyze feature dependencies and blast radius

git-pr-graph-compare.name: Git PR Graph Compare
git-pr-graph-compare.description: Compare spec-graph.json or code-graph.json between PR branch and base branch to show what changed in the specification during development

git-pr-graph-compare-config.description: Requires git repository with graph file at same path in both refs. Uses -g flag to specify graph path. Extracts base version via git show <ref>:<path>.

graph-diff-output.description: GraphDiff output structure with sections: added/removed/modified entities, added/removed/modified dependencies, added/removed references, changed meta fields. Summary counts for each category.

git-ref-resolution.description: Resolves git refs (main, origin/main, HEAD~1) to graph file content. Uses git rev-parse for repo root, git show for file extraction. Tempfile cleanup via finally block ensures no leaked files.

git-pr-diff-complete.description: Successfully shows added/removed/modified entities, dependencies, and references between base and current. Exit 0 on success, 1 on error. Supports both terminal and JSON output formats.

cli-graph-diff-base.description: Command: know graph diff --base <ref> [--verbose] [--format terminal|json]. Extends existing graph diff with optional --base flag. Graph path from -g flag. Mutually exclusive with GRAPH2 positional arg.

git-repo-required.description: Must be inside git repository. Graph file must exist at same path in base ref. File path must be relative to git root. No cross-repo comparison support.
